title: If and Else Statements
description: Make conditional decisions in your code.

slides.title: Making Choices 🤔
slides.content:
Code rarely runs straight from top to bottom. It needs to react to different situations.

The most basic way to direct code flow is an `if` statement.

slides.title: The 'If' Block
slides.content: It evaluates a condition inside parentheses `()`. If it evaluates to `true`, the code inside the curly braces `{}` runs.

quizData.question: What type of value must be produced inside the parentheses `()` of an `if` statement?
quizData.explanation: Conditions are always evaluated down to a boolean `true` or `false`.

slides.title: Adding Else
slides.content: You can add an `else if` for secondary conditions, and a final `else` for when everything else is false.

slides.title: Truthiness 🌊
slides.content: If you just put a variable in the `if(variable)`, JS/TS checks for 'truthiness'. Empty strings `""`, the number `0`, `null`, and `undefined` will evaluate to `false` automatically!
